# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.132.0/http/server.ts";
const port = 8000; // or get the environment variable PORT if it's set, otherwise use 8000 as a default value (Deno Deploy uses this)
import { readLines } from "https://deno.land/std@0.132.0/io/mod.ts"; // Deno std module to handle file operations like reading lines of text files in the filesystem 
const modulesPath = new URL(Deno.env.get("MODULES_PATH") || "http://localhost:8001");// get MODULES_PATH from environment or default localhost port else use it as a module path (this is set by Deno Deploy)  
  const modulesDirectory = new URL(modulesPath, import.meta.url).pathname; // create url object with the given base and appending to /mods/ so we can load from there later on in code   
const allowedExtensionsRegexp = /\.(jsx?|tsx?)$/i ;// regex for handling js or ts files 
let modules: Record<string, any> = {}; // dynamic import() result. Initially empty to hold all loaded module names and their corresponding values  
async function loadModulesFromFile(filePath) {    const fileContentsLinesIterable =  readLines(Deno.open(new URL('./' + modulesDirectory  + '/',import.meta.url).href+'/dist/client').read() );  // open and iterate over the contents of files in 'mods'.  
await Promise.all([...fileContentsLinesIterable].map((line) => {    const filePath = line;     if (allowedExtensionsRegexp.test(filePath)) return import('./' + modulesDirectory  + '/dist/client').then(()=>{  // only process .js/.ts files  
const moduleName='/'.concat(__filename,'../',modulesDir,line) ;// create the full path to this file and add it into our array of loaded mods.    return Deno.load(modulePath); })}))).filter((mod): (typeof import("./" + modulesDirectory  + "/dist/client").ModuleEntryPoint["default"] | undefined ) => {  // filter out any that were not correctly imported  
return typeof imp === 'object' && mod !== null; }));    return filteredModules;}(); Promise.all(loaded_modules).then(()=>{ console.log("All modules loaded!"); }) });});})()} catch (e) {  // handle any errors that occur during file reading or importing  
console.error('Failed to load module:', e)};
